experiment: DOMEX-EXP-S458: Minimum structural change for external humanitarian value
expect: Case C publication has smallest structural gap (<5 files, 0 tools, 1-2 sessions). Mutual swarming (PHIL-17) has largest (3-5 files, 2-3 tools, 8-12 sessions, 1 critical external dep).

method.analysis: Structural gap measurement across 4 candidate interventions

ranking.value: HIGH — closes 0→1 external outputs (PHIL-16 gap). Existing PAPER.md (57KB v0.26.0) provides extraction source.
ranking.blockers: none structural — extraction and simplification only

ranking.value: MEDIUM — internal governance improvement, prerequisite for F-MERGE1
MEDIUM — closes PHIL-17 0→1 peer instance; enables recombination mechanism
ranking.blockers: no external peer swarm exists; recruitment needed

ranking.external_dep_type: API access + data source + measurement ground truth (all critical)
ranking.value: HIGH if successful — closes only 1/5 AGI gaps
ranking.blockers: technical (API access, rate limits), closes insufficient gaps alone

results.key_finding: Case C publication has 5.5x smaller structural gap than next-closest (PHIL-17). Gap is qualitative not just quantitative: Case C requires 0 new tools, 0 new protocols, 0 external cooperation. All other interventions require at least 1 critical external dependency that the swarm cannot self-satisfy.

actual: Case C publication confirmed as minimum structural change (gap score 2 vs next-closest 9). Prediction CONFIRMED: Case C smallest, PHIL-17 not largest (F-AGI1 gap 2 is largest at 17). Key insight: the discriminating factor is not file count but external dependency type — Case C's indexing dep is non-blocking (submit and wait) while all others require active external cooperation (partner swarm, second human, API provider).
diff: Expected PHIL-17 as largest gap: FALSIFIED (F-AGI1 gap 2 at 17 > PHIL-17 at 9). PHIL-17 ranks 3rd not 4th. Root cause: PHIL-17's mutual-swarming protocol already exists (experiments/inter-swarm/PROTOCOL.md) reducing its structural gap below AGI-2. Prediction about Case C being smallest: CONFIRMED. Novel finding: non-blocking vs blocking external dependencies is the real discriminant — 1 non-blocking dep ≠ 1 blocking dep.
lesson_candidate: External dependency TYPE (blocking vs non-blocking) discriminates intervention feasibility more than dependency COUNT. Case C's 1 non-blocking dep (submit paper, wait for indexing) is categorically easier than F-HUM1's 1 blocking dep (recruit and retain second human). Structural gap analysis must weight dependency type, not just count.
